simSettings.title: SIM Settings Configuration
simSettings.description: Configure the simulation environment settings for testing this hypothesis
simSettings.descriptionDetail: Configure the agent classes and environment modules that will be used to test this hypothesis in the simulation experiment. These settings define the simulation environment for validating your research hypothesis.

agentClasses.title: Agent Classes
agentClasses.description: Select the agent classes that will participate in the simulation to test this hypothesis.
agentClasses.hint: Click on a card to view full description

envModules.title: Environment Modules
envModules.description: Select the environment modules that will be used to create the simulation environment for testing this hypothesis.
envModules.hint: Click on a card to view full description

simSettings.loading: Loading agent classes and environment modules...

note.title: Note:
note.text1: The selected agent classes and environment modules define the simulation environment that will be used to test and validate this hypothesis.
note.text2: These settings determine what types of agents will participate in the simulation and what environmental conditions they will interact with.
note.text3: Changes are saved to your simulation settings file automatically.

chat.title: AI Assistant
chat.placeholder: Enter your question or request... (Shift+Enter for new line)

prefillParams.title: Prefill Parameters
prefillParams.introHintBody: Shows available environment modules and agent types with your saved default parameters. The tree below is read-only—use Open prefill_params.json to edit. Custom entries can use Test to verify they load correctly.
prefillParams.selectClass: Select a class to view its prefill parameters

classInfo.noPrefillParams: This class does not have any prefill parameters configured.

errorMessages.backendDisconnected: Backend service is not connected, please start the backend service first

replay.noDataHint: This experiment doesn't have any recorded simulation steps. Make sure the simulation was run with replay recording enabled.

left.selectAgent: Select an Agent on the map to view details
left.name: Name

right.selectTableHint: Select a table to view data

placeholder.noLocationHint: If the simulation doesn’t use a location-aware environment, the map may have no coordinates
placeholder.mapIntegrationHint: For interactive maps with DeckGL + Mapbox, install dependencies:

configPage.title: AI Social Scientist Configuration
configPage.subtitle: Configure your workspace step by step
configPage.description: Complete the environment setup step by step. Each step can be saved independently.

analysis.title: Data Analysis LLM
analysis.hint: For data analysis, insight generation, and report writing (API key/base can be left blank to reuse the default LLM configuration above)
analysis.apiKeyPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM key above
analysis.apiBasePlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM API base URL above
analysis.modelPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM model above (current: {{model}})

python.title: Python Environment
python.pathPlaceholder: python3, or leave empty to auto-detect

configPage.noWorkspaceHint: Open a folder first (File → Open Folder)

llm.apiKeyRequiredHint: For core functions like default chat, analysis, etc.

backend.title: Backend Service Configuration
backend.hint: Port will be auto-allocated, Python path auto-detects if empty

coder.title: Code Generation LLM (optional)
coder.hint: Dedicated model for code generation; leave blank to reuse the default LLM configuration above
coder.apiKeyPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM key above
coder.apiBase: Code Generation LLM API Base URL
coder.apiBasePlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM API base URL above
coder.modelPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM model above (current: {{model}})

embedding.title: Embedding Model
embedding.hint: For text embedding (API key/base can be left blank to reuse the default LLM configuration above)
embedding.apiKeyPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM key above
embedding.apiBasePlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM API base URL above

nano.title: High-Frequency LLM (optional)
nano.hint: Lightweight model for high-frequency operations; leave blank to reuse the default LLM configuration above
nano.apiKeyPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM key above
nano.apiBasePlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM API base URL above
nano.modelPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM model above (current: {{model}})

easypaper.title: EasyPaper (optional)
easypaper.llmApiKeyPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM key above
easypaper.llmModelPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM model above (current: {{model}})
easypaper.vlmModelPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM model above (current: {{model}})
easypaper.vlmApiKeyPlaceholder: Leave blank (it will default to the LLM API key after saving)

finish.title: Configuration Complete!
finish.description: All configurations have been saved to the workspace's .env file.
finish.hint: Click the button below to start the backend service.

notifications.saveSuccessDesc: Configuration has been saved to .env file
notifications.llmKeyRequiredDesc: Please enter LLM API key to continue
notifications.apiBaseMissing: Please enter LLM API Base URL

linkedPlaceholders.apiBase: Leave blank to reuse the default LLM API base URL above (current: {{base}})

validation.emptyApiKey: API Key or Base URL is empty

skillMarketplace.title: Skill Marketplace
skillMarketplace.subtitle: Discover and install curated skills
skillMarketplace.uninstallConfirmContent: Are you sure you want to uninstall this skill?
skillMarketplace.author: Author
skillMarketplace.installLocationHint: Choose where to install skills: workspace (current project only) or global (available for all projects)

skillManagement.title: Skill Management
skillManagement.subtitle: Manage Agent skills used in simulation and Claude folder skills. Toggles apply immediately; turning off does not delete files on disk.
skillManagement.agentTabIntroBody: Same capabilities the simulation uses. “Built-in” ships with the product; “Workspace” lists skills you imported or installed from the marketplace. The toggle controls whether a skill loads at runtime. Archive moves a skill out of regular use without deleting its files.
skillManagement.claudeTabIntroBody: One combined list: bundled templates appear first—use Sync to workspace to copy them into the project. Other rows are folders already on your machine. Syncing does not remove skills you installed from the marketplace.
skillManagement.purgeClaudeConfirmContent: This removes “{{name}}” from disk. You cannot turn it back on from this page. To hide it temporarily, use Deactivate instead.
skillManagement.archiveAgentConfirmContent: “{{name}}” will be archived and taken out of regular use. Files stay in your workspace; to use it again, move the folder back and run Scan workspace for agent skills.
skillManagement.claudeWorkspaceExplain: Applies only to the folder you have open; marketplace installs and imports usually land here.
skillManagement.claudeGlobalExplain: Shared across projects—good for skills you use everywhere.
skillManagement.vsixTemplateExplain: Sample copies included with the extension for preview; use Sync to workspace to use them in this project.

marketplaceErr.noSourcesAgent: No Agent marketplace sources yet. Open “Agent marketplace sources” in this panel and add GitHub repositories; installs go under custom/skills.
marketplaceErr.noSourcesClaude: No Claude marketplace sources yet. Open “Claude marketplace sources”—independent from Agent—and add repos; installs go under .claude/skills.
marketplaceErr.network: Couldn’t reach the skill marketplace. Check your connection and try again.
marketplaceErr.sourceFailed: Couldn’t load source “{{source}}”. Try again in a moment.

skillManagement.deleteConfirmContent: Are you sure you want to delete skill "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
skillManagement.importClaudeSuccess: Imported into this project’s Claude skill folder
skillManagement.noAgentSkills: No agent skills yet. Refresh or check the messages above.
skillManagement.noClaudeCodeSkills: No Claude folder skills or bundled templates yet. Install from the marketplace or sync a template.
skillManagement.noMarketplaceSkills: No marketplace items (check configuration and messages above)
skillManagement.noMarketplaceSkillsAgent: No Agent marketplace items yet. Confirm sources under “Agent marketplace sources” and review the messages above.
skillManagement.noMarketplaceSkillsClaude: No Claude marketplace items yet. Confirm sources under “Claude marketplace sources” (separate from Agent) and review the messages above.
skillManagement.syncExtensionSkills: Copy bundled templates into this project
skillManagement.marketplaceAgentHint: Use “Agent marketplace sources” below to add GitHub repos; installs go to custom/skills. Up to 10 items per page—paginate at the bottom.
skillManagement.marketplaceClaudeHint: Use “Claude marketplace sources” below—separate from Agent. Installs go to .claude/skills. Up to 10 items per page.
skillManagement.marketplaceRefreshHint: Refresh updates both lists below. Marketplace source buttons add or adjust GitHub repos.
skillManagement.sourcesConfigHint: Configure remote repository sources for the skill marketplace. Supports GitHub, GitLab, and Gitee.
skillManagement.configureSourcesHint: Add marketplace sources in the Advanced Settings below
skillManagement.sourcesConfigHintWithDefault: Configure remote repository sources for the skill marketplace. Built-in sources are listed below (marked as "Built-in"); you can add or modify them. Supports GitHub, GitLab, and Gitee.
skillManagement.resetToDefaultTooltip: Reset to built-in default sources (anthropics/skills, obra/superpowers, affaan-m/everything-claude-code)
skillManagement.defaultSourcesHint: Built-in default sources are listed below and loaded automatically.
skillManagement.helpAgentTabDesc: Manage skills used by Agent simulation runtime.
skillManagement.helpAgentTabDescFull: Same capabilities the simulation uses. "Built-in" ships with the product; "Workspace" lists skills you imported or installed from the marketplace. The toggle controls whether a skill loads at runtime. Archive moves a skill out of regular use without deleting its files.
skillManagement.helpAgentEnable: Enable/Disable: Toggle whether the skill is loaded at runtime.
skillManagement.helpAgentArchive: Archive: Move skill out of regular directory and stop using it. Files remain on disk.
skillManagement.helpAgentImport: Import: Import new Agent skills from local folders.
skillManagement.helpAgentScan: Scan Workspace: Rescan workspace directories to discover new skills.
skillManagement.helpClaudeTabDesc: Manage skills written to Claude configuration directory.
skillManagement.helpClaudeTabDescFull: One combined list: bundled templates appear first—use Sync to workspace to copy them into the project. Other rows are folders already on your machine. Syncing does not remove skills you installed from the marketplace.
skillManagement.helpClaudeSync: Sync to Workspace: Copy bundled templates into current project.
skillManagement.helpClaudeToggle: Enable/Disable: Control whether Claude loads this skill directory.
skillManagement.helpClaudeDelete: Delete Permanently: Remove skill files from disk, cannot be undone. Use "Disable" to hide it temporarily.
skillManagement.helpMarketplaceDesc: Discover and install skills from remote repositories.
skillManagement.helpMarketplaceInstall: Install: Choose to install as Agent skill (custom/skills) or Claude skill (.claude/skills).
skillManagement.helpMarketplaceSources: Advanced Settings: Configure marketplace source repositories (supports GitHub, GitLab, Gitee). Built-in sources are loaded automatically.
skillManagement.helpMarketplaceUpdate: Update: Installed skills show "Update available" tag when new versions exist.
skillManagement.helpSourcesDesc: Add or modify skill source repositories in Advanced Settings. Built-in sources (anthropics/skills, obra/superpowers, affaan-m/everything-claude-code) are loaded by default. If you encounter GitHub API rate limits, configure agentSkills.githubToken in VSCode settings.
skillManagement.githubTokenDesc: Optional. Fill in your GitHub Personal Access Token to authenticate API requests and avoid the anonymous rate limit (60 req/hour). Only public_repo scope is needed.
